observations.recommendation: 3. To apply the guidelines and procedures on inventory taking in accordance with section 3.0 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
6. To Conduct the Physical Inventory taking pursuant to Section 5.3 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
7. To prepare the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE) in pursuant of Section 6.2.13 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
8. To ensure that the recording are updated pursuant to Section 6.3.1(a) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
10. To prepare list of non-existing/missing PPEs in accordance with Section 6.3.1(d) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
11. To prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) in accordance with Section 6.3.1(h) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
12. To reconcile with the Accounting Unit in accordance with Section 6.3.1(f) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

14. To take up necessary accounting entries to recognize the loss of PPE and to set up corresponding receivables from concerned accountable officers pursuant to Section 6.3.2(b) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
15. To reconcile with the Property Unit, pursuant to Section 6.3.2(c) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
16. To take up necessary accounting entries to write-off/drop from the books of accounts the remaining balance as of December 31, 2021, pursuant to Section 6.3.2(e) of COA Circular No. 2020-006.

17. To ensure the total balance of PPELCs/SLs totally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledgers, Pursuant to Section 6.3.2(f) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
18. To witness the conduct of the inventory in accordance with Section 5.5 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

1.14 In light of the aforementioned, we recommended that the LCE instruct:

1.14.1 The Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of all the
LGU’s PPE and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE, which
must be submitted to the Office of the Auditor by January 31 of
each year, pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs
Volume I.

1.14.2 The Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to keep
track of their individual property cards. Afterwards, reconcile
their records, amend each property ledger card, and reflect the
right PPE account valuation in the current year's Statement of
Financial Position, in accordance with Sections 119 and 120 of
the MNGAS for LGUs Volume I and Sections 13 and 45 of the
same Manual Volume II.

2.9 Therefore, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):

2.9.1 Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of
its road networks in order to ascertain the existence of the
balance of the Road Network Accounts, pursuant to Sections
V.1 and V.2 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No.
2015-008 dated November 23, 2015;
2.9.2 Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal
Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of
the road networks to facilitate the preparation of the subsidiary
ledgers for roads and its components;
2.9.3 Require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain
the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its
components, including Road Map, and as well as the
preparation of Report on Local Road Networks.

3.7 We recommended that the LCE:
3.7.1 Demand that the Municipal Engineer to complete the required
Status of Work Accomplished for each infrastructure project
that management implements. This document will be verified by
the Municipality's Inspectorate Committee and serve as the foundation
for the issuance of the corresponding Certificates of
Completion and Acceptance by the head of agency.
3.7.2 Require that the Status of Work Accomplished, Inspection
Reports, and Certificates of Completion and Acceptance on
Projects / Programs under the 20% Development Fund be
submitted to the Office of the Auditor, in accordance with
Section 2.1 of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017 and Appendix 1 of
the RIRR of RA 9184, respectively.

4.7 We recommended that the LCE to:
4.7.1 Instruct the Bid and Award Committee to provide the audit
team of the perfected contracts, purchase orders and all
supporting documentation within five working days from the
date of its issuance. This will allow the Auditor time to review
the contracts and purchase orders and identify to management
any defects and/or deficiencies, if any, in accordance with
Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2009-001; and
4.7.2 Submit the contract for technical inspection and evaluation by
the COA Technical Audit Specialist (TAS), to ensure the
regularity and propriety of the contracts.

5.5 We recommended for the effort of the management to remit the
mandatory deductions/withheld to the GFIs such as BIR, GSIS, PagIBIG
and PHIC, in accordance with Section 81 of the National Revenue Code,
Section 3.4 Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRRs) of
the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 8291, otherwise known as the GSIS Act of
1997, Section 3 of the IRRs of the RA No. 9679, otherwise known as the
HDMF of 2009 on PAG-IBIG, Section II of the IRRs of the RA No. 7875,
otherwise known as the National Insurance Act, respectively.

1. We recommend that the Inventory Committee be convened and for them to immediately schedule the conduct of the annual inventory of all assets to ensure fairness of accounts presentation in the financial statements. In the preparation of the report, the different column should be properly fill-up to ensure that all items are properly identified and accounted for. Assignment of Property Number should be properly done so that each of the items has a unique code for easy reference. Likewise, the Municipal accountant and the Property Municipal Treasurer should maintain the required stock cards, equipment ledger cards and property cards respectively. In addition, when the physical inventory of PPE is already available it shall be reconciled with the (a) PPE Ledger Card, (b) the control accounts and (c) stock and property cards. If there would be any discrepancy, it should be immediately verified and adjusted.

2. Require the Municipal Government to strictly adhere to prescribed procurement laws and regulations on Section 373 of Republic Act No. 7160 otherwise known as the Local Government Code of the Philippines and IRR RA No. 9184, the Government Procurement Reform Act.

4. To create the Inventory Committee Composing of Accounting, Property Division/Units and Internal Audit Service/Unit for the purpose of cleansing the PPE Account in accordance with Section 5.2 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
5. To prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) in accordance with Section 5.9 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
9. To prepare Property Cards for items found at station, in accordance with Section 6.3.1(c) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

13. To take necessary accounting entries to recognize PPEs found at the station and prepare corresponding Property, Plant Equipment Ledger Cards based in the List of PPEs found at the Station in accordance with Section 6.3.2(a) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

19. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to require the accountable officer of the Government responsible for the activities that require the execution of the contract shall immediately furnish copies to the COA and other Offices concerned which must be within five (5) days from the date of execution as prescribed by COA Circular No. 96-010.

20. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive that the accountable officer responsible for the activities that require the execution of the contract shall immediately furnish copies to the COA and other Offices concerned which must be within five (5) days from the date of execution as prescribed by COA Circular No. 96-010.

5. Require the Municipal Government to strictly adhere to prescribed procurement laws and regulations on Section 373 of Republic Act No. 7160 otherwise known as the Local Government Code of the Philippines and IRR RA No. 9184, the Government Procurement Reform Act. We recommended that officials responsible for the activities that require the execution of contract shall immediately furnish copies to the COA and other offices concerned which must be within five (5) days from the date of execution as prescribed by COA Circular No. 96-010.
observations.reason: 5. Due to the distance of the Municipal LGU from the PSAO

observations.recommendation:
1.12 We recommend to the management the following:
a. Local Chief Executive
a.1 To apply the guidelines and procedures on inventory taking
in accordance with section 3.0 of the Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
a.2 To create the Inventory Committee Composing of
Accounting, Property Division/Units and Internal Audit
Service/Unit for the purpose of cleansing the PPE Account in
accordance with Section 5.2 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020.
b. Inventory Committee
b.1 To prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) in
accordance with Section 5.9 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020.
b.2 To Conduct the Physical Inventory taking pursuant to
Section 5.3 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No.
2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
b.3 To prepare the Report on Physical Count of Property,
Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE) in pursuant of Section 6.2.13
of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
c. Property Unit
c.1 To ensure that the recording are updated pursuant to
Section 6.3.1(a) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020.
c.2 To prepare Property Cards for items found at station, in
accordance with Section 6.3.1(c) of the COA Circular No.
2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
c.3 To prepare list of non-existing/missing PPEs in accordance
with Section (d) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020.
c.4 To prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) in accordance with Section
6.3.1(h) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
2020.
c.5 To reconcile with the Accounting Unit in accordance with
Section 6.3.1(f) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020.
d. Accounting Unit
d.1 To take necessary accounting entries to recognize PPEs
found at the station and prepare corresponding Property,
Plant Equipment Ledger Cards based in the List of PPEs
found at the Station in accordance with Section 6.3.2(a) of the
COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
d.2 To take up necessary accounting entries to recognize the
loss of PPE and to set up corresponding receivables from
concerned accountable officers pursuant to Section 6.3.2(b) of
the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
d.3 To reconcile with the Property Unit, pursuant to Section
6.3.2(c) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
2020.
d.4 To take up necessary accounting entries to write-off/drop
from the books of accounts the remaining balance as of
December 31, 2021, pursuant to Section 6.3.2(e) of COA
Circular No. 2020-006
d.5 To ensure the total balance of PPELCs/SLs totally with the
balances of controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledgers,
Pursuant to Section 6.3.2(f) of the COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020.
e. Internal Audit Service/Unit
e.1 To witness the conduct of the inventory in accordance with
Section 5.5 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January
31, 2020.

2.3 We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to require the
accountable officer of the Municipal Government to strictly adhere to
the prescribed procurement laws and regulations on Section 373 of
Republic Act No. 7160 otherwise known as the Local Government
Code of the Philippines and IRR RA No. 9184, the Government
Procurement Reform Act and furnish the team with the copy or
copies of the APP.

3.3 We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal
government to strictly adhere to the prescribed procurement laws and
regulations on Section 373 of Republic Act No. 7160 otherwise known
as the Local Government Code of the Philippines and IRR RA No.
9184, the Government Procurement Reform Act. We recommend that
officials responsible for the activities that require the execution of the
contract shall immediately furnish copies to the Commission on Audit
(COA) and other offices concerned which must be within five (5) days
from the date of execution as prescribed by Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 96-010.

1. We recommend that the Inventory Committee be convened and for them to afterwards was schedule the conduct of the annual inventory of all assets to ensure fairness of accounts presentation in the Municipality’s financial statements. In the preparation of the report, the different column should be properly fill-up to ensure that all items are properly identified and accounted for. Assignment of Property Number should be properly done so that each of the items has a unique code for easy reference. Likewise, the Municipal accountant and the Municipal Custodian/ Municipal Treasurer should maintain the required stock cards, equipment ledger cards and property cards respectively. In addition, when the physical inventory of PPE is already available it shall be reconciled with the (a) PPE Ledger Card, ( b) the control accounts and (c) stock and property cards. If there would be any discrepancy, it should be immediately verified and adjusted.

observations.reason: 1. Due to the effect of Pandemic.

observations.recommendation: 2. We recommended that management stop the practice of incurring obligations without the corresponding cash back-up to avoid penalties, surcharges and most of all legal implications in case the municipality would be unable to meet its obligations.

3. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to require the attachment of the Waste Material Report in all payment of repairs and maintenance of their machinery and similar nature as part of the supporting documents.

4. We recommended to require the property officer to renew Memorandum Receipts and/ or Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment using the new format in accordance with the instructions under COA Handbook on Property and Supply Management System.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal
Treasurer to exert extra effort to collect the Real Property Tax Receivables in
accordance with Section 6 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-832
dated July 3, 1992.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the
accountable officer/s to conduct inventory taking on all the LGU’s Property, Plant
and Equipment in accordance with Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA)
Circular No. 92-382 dated October 20, 1992.

We recommend to the Management to allocate the fund necessary for the
Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) and judiciously
spend it in accordance with Section 21 of the Republic Act No. 10121, otherwise
known as an Act Strengthening the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction
Management System, providing for the National Disaster Risk and Management
Framework and institutionalizing the National Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Plan Appropriating fund thereof and other purposes.

We recommend to the management to allocate sufficient funds for the
preparation of the Solid Waste Management Plan in accordance to Section 16 of the
Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of
1991.

44. We recognized the Local Chief Executive (LCE) for the supervision and monitoring of the Local Risk and Reduction Management Fund resulted on its implementation, pursuant to Section 39 of Republic Act (RA) No. 10717, otherwise known as the FY 2016 General Appropriation Act.

1. We recommend that Head of Agency should create a committee to conduct complete physical inventory of PPE at least once a year pursuant to COA Circular No. 80-124 January 18, 1980, Section 490 of GAAM Vol. 1 and Section 58 of PD 1445. Report of the Physical Count of PPE. Any discrepancies between physical count and inventories per books must be analyzed, investigated and corresponding course of action be taken up immediately. Report of the RPCPPE shall be submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.

2. We recommend that management strictly implement sound and effective internal control and such other measures to ensure prompt reporting, remittances and deposit of collection in order to reflect the correct cash balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: 2. The management implemented internal control and such other measures.

observations.recommendation: 3. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant and Disbursing Officer to give priority to the preparation and submission of the required reports, to facilitate timely review and verification of transaction, pursuant to Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

4. We recommend that management will instruct the personnel concerned to settle their respective notice of suspension and comply to the auditing rules and regulations on the documentary requirements for transactions in order to avoid similar suspensions in the future.
observations.managementAction: 4. Managem ent demanded the personnel concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension.

observations.recommendation: 5. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Accountable Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit the Cashbooks on Cash in Treasury, Cash Advances, Bank Reconciliation Statement with the Bank Statements and Subsidiary Ledgers, pursuant to Section 100 of Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: 5. Accountable officer submitted the Cashbook and the audit team was able to conduct cash examination until June 2016.

observations.recommendation: 6. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel assigned to submit the listing of the real property tax declarants, amount, date due and subsidiary ledgers, pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

7. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to apply the Depreciation and the necessary adjusting entries, in accordance with paragraph 59 of Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard No.17 on the Property, Plant and Equipment: Depreciation–Office Equipment; Depreciation–Land Transportation; Depreciation–Furniture & Fixture. To record suspension on Office Equipment, Land Transportation and Furniture and Fixture.
observations.managementAction: 7. The Municipal Accountant prepared the JEV with the corresponding adjusting entries of Depreciation.

observations.recommendation: 8. We recommended to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension, if not pursued to Section 9.4 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: 8. Management instructed the personnel concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension.

observations.recommendation: 9. We recommend to the management to submit the required reports within the prescribed period, pursuant to Sections 100 and 122 of Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: 9. Management was able to submit the reports in CY 2016.
